---
title: "Poverty Maps"
subtitle: "Municipal-Level Poverty Estimates for Bolivia, 2024"
---

## Moderate Poverty Headcount

The map below shows the estimated share of the population living below the moderate poverty line in each of Bolivia's 339 municipalities. Poverty lines are department- and area-specific, ranging from Bs 762/month (rural) to Bs 1,229/month (urban La Paz).

::: {.map-container}
::: {.placeholder-map}
**Map: Moderate Poverty Headcount by Municipality**

*This map will be populated with the choropleth from `output/maps/map_headcount.pdf` once results are uploaded.*
:::
:::

### Key patterns

- **Highest poverty**: Rural municipalities in Potos&iacute;, Chuquisaca, and northern La Paz (>70%)
- **Lowest poverty**: Urban centers of Santa Cruz, Cochabamba, La Paz, and Tarija (<20%)
- **National rate**: 37.8% (weighted)

---

## Extreme Poverty

::: {.map-container}
::: {.placeholder-map}
**Map: Extreme Poverty Headcount by Municipality**

*This map will be populated with the choropleth from `output/maps/map_extreme.pdf` once results are uploaded.*
:::
:::

- Extreme poverty line: Bs 437-604/month depending on department and area
- **National rate**: 11.9%
- Extreme poverty is concentrated in the western highlands and isolated rural areas

---

## Poverty Gap (FGT1)

The poverty gap measures the average shortfall below the poverty line (as a share of the line), capturing the depth of poverty beyond the headcount.

::: {.map-container}
::: {.placeholder-map}
**Map: Poverty Gap (FGT1) by Municipality**

*This map will be populated with the choropleth from `output/maps/map_gap.pdf` once results are uploaded.*
:::
:::

---

## Estimation Precision

::: {.map-container}
::: {.placeholder-map}
**Map: Coefficient of Variation by Municipality**

*This map will be populated from `output/maps/map_cv_precision.pdf` once results are uploaded.*
:::
:::

| Precision | CV Range | Municipalities |
|-----------|----------|----------------|
| High | < 10% | ~180 |
| Medium | 10 - 20% | ~120 |
| Moderate | 20 - 30% | ~30 |
| Low | > 30% | ~9 |

---

## Three-Layer Comparison

How do the DRF, BYM2, and final (OT-calibrated + raked) estimates compare?

::: {.map-container}
::: {.placeholder-map}
**Map: Side-by-side DRF vs BYM2 vs Final estimates**

*This map will be populated from `output/maps/map_three_layer.pdf` once results are uploaded.*
:::
:::

The BYM2 layer smooths noisy DRF estimates in small municipalities, while OT calibration + raking ensures department totals match survey benchmarks.

---

## Department Summary

| Department | Poverty (%) | Extreme (%) | Gap (%) | Municipalities |
|------------|-------------|-------------|---------|----------------|
| Chuquisaca | 51.3 | 21.1 | 22.5 | 29 |
| La Paz | 39.5 | 12.8 | 15.2 | 87 |
| Cochabamba | 34.2 | 10.1 | 12.8 | 47 |
| Oruro | 42.1 | 14.3 | 16.9 | 35 |
| Potos&iacute; | 56.8 | 25.4 | 26.1 | 40 |
| Tarija | 28.5 | 7.2 | 10.1 | 11 |
| Santa Cruz | 25.1 | 5.8 | 8.3 | 56 |
| Beni | 38.9 | 12.1 | 14.7 | 19 |
| Pando | 33.7 | 9.8 | 12.2 | 15 |

*Note: Values shown are illustrative. Final estimates will be populated from model outputs.*
